interpretation: The curve is broadly upward-sloping but exhibits a hump, with the 2Y yield (11.95%) exceeding the 5Y (10.82%) and 10Y (11.15%), while the 15Y peaks at 12.68%. The 364D dip below 182D suggests near-term liquidity pockets. Elevated short-end yields above the 10% repo rate reflect tight banking sector liquidity and BB's contractionary stance to combat persistent inflation (hovering ~10%). The 2Y bulge indicates market expectations of prolonged monetary tightening before eventual easing, while the steep long end prices in fiscal supply pressure from large NSD/T-bond issuance.
implications: Banks face negative carry funding government securities at short tenors, compressing NIM and discouraging primary dealer participation in 1-5Y segments
The 2Y hump signals market expectation that BB will maintain or raise the repo rate through mid-2027, with inflation unlikely to retreat to the 6% target soon
Steep 10Y-20Y segment (50bps) reflects fiscal deficit financing concerns and duration risk premium amid uncertainty over government borrowing plans and potential sovereign rating pressures
